subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Wolf-Rayet Stars: Cosmic Blowtorches
description: Learn Wolf-Rayet Stars: Cosmic Blowtorches in one minute.

hook.text: Wolf-Rayet stars are so hot and luminous that they blow away their own outer layers in fierce stellar winds.

buildup.text: Temperatures reach 200,000°C at the surface. They lose a Sun's worth of mass every 100,000 years.

discovery.text: Their winds create glowing bubbles and shells of expelled gas, visible in telescopes as cosmic sculptures.

twist.text: They're the final stage before a massive star explodes. Every Wolf-Rayet star is a supernova waiting to happen.

climax.text: Only about 500 are known in our galaxy. They're rare because this phase lasts just a few hundred thousand years.

punchline.text: Stars so hot they tear themselves apart. The last act before the blast.

quiz.question: What makes Wolf-Rayet stars unique among massive stars?
options: They are blowing away their own outer layers in powerful stellar winds
They are the coldest supergiant stars known
They orbit black holes and feed material into them
